etymology.description: 说(說) (shuō, "speak") means to say or speak, typically representing verbal communication. 话(話) (huà, "words; speech") refers to spoken words or language, derived from a character that includes the speech radical, indicating a relation to spoken communication.
etymology.interpretation: 说(說) (shuō, "speak") is the act of talking, where one uses verbal speech to communicate ideas. 话(話) (huà, "words; speech") refers to the content or unit of speech, representing the medium or form of spoken expression. Together, 说话(說話) combines the action of speaking with the words spoken, directly pointing to the act of communicating speech or engaging in conversation.

examples.chinese: 孩子们在花园里说话(孩子們在花園裏說話)。
examples.english: The children are speaking in the garden.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢在大会上说话(她喜歡在大會上說話)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài dàhuì shàng shuōhuà.
examples.english: She likes to speak at conferences.

examples.chinese: 我们不应该在图书馆大声说话(我們不應該在圖書館大聲說話)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bù yīnggāi zài túshūguǎn dàshēng shuōhuà.
examples.english: We should not speak loudly in the library.
